title: `phase5_custom` primary needs a pseudocode→Python materialization step before full audit
source: Loop A tick 11 surfaced this — T011D2 cleared lint, two rounds of DA-feedback retry (B0037), final DA verdict PROCEED_WITH_CAVEAT, and pre-flight passed (via B0038 + B0039 fixes). Then the full audit subprocess crashed with `ValueError: Phase 5 custom primary 'phase5_custom' not found at pipeline.primaries_phase5.phase5_custom: No module named ...`. [scripts/run_xau_d1.py:75-84](scripts/run_xau_d1.py#L75-L84) requires an importable module at `pipeline.primaries_phase5.<primary_name>` exposing a `signal(ohlcv, features, cfg) -> pd.Series` function. The hypothesizer emits `primary: phase5_custom` with a `custom_primary_pseudocode` STRING describing the rule (e.g., "rolling-quantile rank of cot_net_noncomm_z52w over 504 bars >= 0.75 AND CUSUM h=2.0×ATR"). The pseudocode is not executable.
why: This is the **third stacked gap** of the Loop A audit-dispatch path (after B0038 schema + B0039 asset-injection) but it is qualitatively deeper: B0038/B0039 were ~5-line orchestration fixes; B0040 is a codegen step. Without it, every Loop A survivor that uses `phase5_custom` (the catch-all primary the hypothesizer picks when none of the four built-in primaries fit) ends at preflight_passed_pending_audit forever — the loop produces candidates that cannot be empirically tested. Existing audit-capable Phase 5 primaries (`phase5_cot_extremes`, `phase5_gld_holdings`, `phase5_gdelt_tone`, `phase5_xau_bearq_conjunction`) were all human-authored against pre-committed specs; nothing in the codebase translates pseudocode to one of those.
value_rationale: H (without one of these options, Loop A produces unaudited candidates indefinitely — the whole point of the audit step is to falsify, not just describe).
recommendation: Option A for T011D2 today (smoke-test the audit path end-to-end with a real survivor; ~20-30 min), then decide between A vs B vs C as a Phase 5.2 design question. Option B + a separate "novel-primary" promotion track may be the right long-term answer.
out_of_scope: changes to the proposal schema (primary_custom_sha256 is already there and would carry the artifact identity for Option C).

history.reason: feat(B0040+B0041+tick12): first end-to-end audit + 4th audit-path gap
feat(B0040+B0041+tick12): first end-to-end audit + 4th audit-path gap

Option B (manual promotion track) chosen by user over Option C (autonomous codegen) — preserves human review of all custom-primary code before it touches the audit. Implementation: phase5/run_proposal.py gains _is_unmaterialized_custom_primary(p) + a gate after pre-flight that parks phase5_* proposals lacking a pipeline/primaries_phase5/<primary>.py module at status=pending_materialization (record carries pseudocode + expected module path + promote_command). Re-running run_proposal after the human writes+reviews the module IS the promotion step — no separate CLI. main() exit 0 for pending_materialization (valid waiting state, not a crash). Docs: .claude/agents/phase5-hypothesizer.md Custom primaries section + CLAUDE.md Loop A section. Tests: tests/phase5/test_b0040_materialization_gate.py (5: helper true/false branches + run() parks custom + does not park builtin). 550/550 tests pass. Unblocks B0035 value (per-episode escalation now reachable once a custom survivor materializes).
feat(phase5): close 7 tickets â€” Loop A/H4 infra hardening + reporting
feat(B0035): cross-episode survival sign test for low-n_episodes regimes
feat(loop-a): B0040 custom-primary materialization->audit path demonstrated; B0085 normalizer; B0086 logged
